prerequisites: A Google Cloud project with the Pub/Sub API enabled; supply its identifier as `project_id`.
A Pub/Sub topic created in that project for the connector to publish into; supply its name as `topic_id`.
A Google Cloud service account granted the Pub/Sub Editor role (publish permission to the topic).
A JSON-formatted service account key file. Airbyte's Google PubSub destination supports JSON keys only; its contents go in the `credentials_json` field.

authMethods.name: Service account JSON key (credentials_json)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Google Cloud service account key in JSON format. Create a service account, grant it the Pub/Sub Editor role (publish access), generate a JSON key, and paste the full key file contents into the `credentials_json` secret field. Only JSON-format keys are supported.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the GCP project and topic
setupSteps.body: In Google Cloud, select or create a project with the Pub/Sub API enabled, and create the Pub/Sub topic you want to write to. Note the project ID for `project_id` and the topic name for `topic_id`.

setupSteps.title: Create a service account with the Pub/Sub Editor role
setupSteps.body: Create a service account and grant it the Pub/Sub Editor role so it can publish messages to the topic. With gcloud: `gcloud iam service-accounts create SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME`, then `gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding PROJECT_ID --member="serviceAccount:SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME@PROJECT_ID.iam.gserviceaccount.com" --role=roles/pubsub.editor`.

setupSteps.title: Generate a JSON service account key
setupSteps.body: In the Cloud Console, open IAM & Admin > Service Accounts, click the service account, go to the Keys tab, choose Add key > Create new key, select JSON, and click Create to download the key file. Alternatively run `gcloud iam service-accounts keys create KEY_FILE --iam-account=SA_NAME@PROJECT_ID.iam.gserviceaccount.com`. The key is downloaded only once, so store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the full contents of the JSON key file into `credentials_json`, and set `project_id` and `topic_id`. Set `ordering_enabled` to control whether message ordering is used and `batching_enabled` to enable batching. When batching is enabled, optionally tune `batching_delay_threshold`, `batching_element_count_threshold`, and `batching_request_bytes_threshold`.
